chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1224 of 2025 Applicant: Sono son of Gullo, through Abdul Basit, Advocate Complainant Respondent:: Muhammad Imran, through Hafiz Muhammad Khan Panhwar, Panhwar The State Through Mr. Qamaruddin, DPG. Date of hearing: 03.06.2025 Date of order: 03.06.2025 O R D E R MUHAMMAD HASAN (AKBAR), J -- Through this Bail Application, the applicant/accused seeks pre-arrest bail in Crime No.197 of 2025 registered under Sections 337-A(i), 337-A(iii), PPC at Police Station NKIA, Karachi, after his bail plea has been declined by the learned IVth Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi-Central, vide order dated 05.05.2025. 2. Brief facts of the case is that on 13.03.2025 at about 1800 hrs, the applicant attacked on the complainant and caused him injuries towards face and nose. 3. When the matter was taken up, Hafiz Muhammad Khan Panhwar, Advocate filed his power on behalf of the Complainant, which is taken on record. The complainant Muhammad Imran son of Muhammad Saddique is also present and filed his affidavit of No Objection, same is taken on record, stating therein that due to intervention of Naik Mard, the complainant has forgiven the applicant in the name of Almighty Allah and they have now compromised with each other. Learned DPG also extends his No Objection if the bail is granted to the Applicant. In view of the above, the instant bail Page 2 of 2 application is allowed and the interim pre-arrest bail granted by this Court to the applicant/accused vide order dated 13.05.2025 is hereby confirmed on same terms and conditions. 4. Needless to mention here that the observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not influence the learned trial Court while deciding the case of the applicants on merits.

In view of the above, the instant bail Page 2 of 2 application is allowed and the interim pre-arrest bail granted by this Court to the applicant/accused vide order dated 13.05.2025 is hereby confirmed on same terms and conditions. 4. Needless to mention here that the observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not influence the learned trial Court while deciding the case of the applicants on merits. JUDGE Hyder/PS*
